messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-planning agent. Prepare a compact context pack for formalizing a LaTeX theorem-like source unit. The target Lean declarations aligned to the selected source unit are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "rules": [
    "Do not infer or reveal hidden target Lean declaration names for the selected unit.",

    "Do not bundle all source parts into one conjunction unless the source unit itself requires that shape.",
    "Use previous project declarations only if they are shown under prior_project_context.",
    "Use local_file_predecessor_declarations only as same-file helper/style context; the selected unit's target declarations are omitted.",
    "Do not treat Mathlib as the only context; enumerate source/project/local/Mathlib context separately.",
    "Prefer exact Mathlib names when known; otherwise give a narrow query plus expected signature shape.",
    "Keep added context tight: prefer a few thousand tokens or less per source unit."
  ],
  "schema": {

  },

The context inventory must be separate: source text, previous book/source statements, previous project declarations, local file/import/style context, and selected Mathlib APIs.",

          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def LatticePath.vertices (path : LatticePath) (start : LatticePoint) : List LatticePoint :=\n  start :: path.scanl (fun p s => s.apply p) start |>.tail\n\n/-- Check if a path goes from point a to point b. -/",

        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def LatticePath.isPathFromTo (path : LatticePath) (a b : LatticePoint) : Prop :=\n  path.endpoint a = b\n\n/-- If a path goes from A to B, then take n goes from A to an intermediate point,\n    and drop n goes from that point to B. -/",
          "line_range": [
            350,

        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def numNipats2 (A A' B B' : LatticePoint) : ℕ :=\n  let AB : kVertex 2 := ![A, A']\n  let BB : kVertex 2 := ![B, B']\n  (nipatsFromTo AB BB).ncard\n\n/-- Helper: the 2x2 determinant expands to the difference of products. -/",
          "line_range": [
            1146,
            1151
          ],
          "name": "numNipats2",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV1.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def isWeaklyNorthwestOf (p q : LatticePoint) : Prop :=\n  p.x ≤ q.x ∧ p.y ≥ q.y\n\n/-- Helper: If a path exists from a to b, then a.1 ≤ b.1 and a.2 ≤ b.2. -/",
          "line_range": [

          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma mem_vertices_cons (s : LatticeStep) (rest : LatticePath) (start : LatticePoint)\n    (v : LatticePoint) :\n    v ∈ LatticePath.vertices (s :: rest) start ↔\n    v = start ∨ v ∈ LatticePath.vertices rest (s.apply start)",
          "line_range": [
            2685,

          ],

        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma vertices_cons_subset (s : LatticeStep) (rest : LatticePath) (start : LatticePoint)\n    (v : LatticePoint) :\n    v ∈ LatticePath.vertices rest (s.apply start) →\n    v ∈ LatticePath.vertices (s :: rest) start",
          "line_range": [
            2693,
            2737
          ],
          "name": "vertices_cons_subset",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV1.lean",

        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem baby_jordan_curve (A A' B B' : LatticePoint)\n    (hA : isWeaklyNorthwestOf A' A) (hB : isWeaklyNorthwestOf B' B)\n    (p : LatticePath) (p' : LatticePath)\n    (hp : p.isPathFromTo A B') (hp' : p'.isPathFromTo A' B) :\n    ∃ v, v ∈ p.vertices A ∧ v ∈ p'.vertices A'",
          "line_range": [
            2738,

          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem no_nipats_under_nw (A A' B B' : LatticePoint)\n    (hA : isWeaklyNorthwestOf A' A) (hB : isWeaklyNorthwestOf B' B) :\n    numNipats2 A A' B' B = 0",
          "line_range": [

      "previous_source_context": [
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "example",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex:exa.lgv.2paths.2",
            "labels": [
              "exa.lgv.2paths.2"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              518,
              533
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [
              "prop.lgv.2paths.count"
            ],
            "source_text": "\\begin{example}\n\\label{exa.lgv.2paths.2}Let $A=\\left(  0,0\\right)  $ and $A^{\\prime}=\\left(\n-1,1\\right)  $ and $B=\\left(  2,2\\right)  $ and $B^{\\prime}=\\left(\n0,3\\right)  $. Then, the claim of Proposition \\ref{prop.lgv.2paths.count}\nsimplifies, since $\\left(  \\text{\\# of nipats from }\\left(  A,A^{\\prime\n}\\right)  \\text{ to }\\left(  B^{\\prime},B\\right)  \\right)  =0$ in this case.\nHere is a picture of the four points that should make this visually clear:\n\\[%\n%TCIMACRO{\\TeXButton{just four points}{% [tikz figure removed]}}%\n%BeginExpansion\n% [tikz figure removed]%\n%EndExpansion\n\\ \\ .\n\\]\n\n\\end{example}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "proposition",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex:prop.lgv.jordan-2",
            "labels": [
              "prop.lgv.jordan-2",
              "eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.1",
              "eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.2"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              836,
              852
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n[baby Jordan curve theorem]\\label{prop.lgv.jordan-2}Let $A$, $B$, $A^{\\prime}$\nand $B^{\\prime}$ be four lattice points satisfying%\n\\begin{align}\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  A^{\\prime}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nA\\right)  ,\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A^{\\prime}\\right)\n\\geq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A\\right)  ,\\label{eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.1}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  B^{\\prime}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nB\\right)  ,\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B^{\\prime}\\right)\n\\geq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B\\right)  . \\label{eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.2}%\n\\end{align}\nHere, $\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  P\\right)  $ and $\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nP\\right)  $ denote the two coordinates of any point $P\\in\\mathbb{Z}^{2}$.\n\nLet $p$ be any path from $A$ to $B^{\\prime}$. Let $p^{\\prime}$ be any path\nfrom $A^{\\prime}$ to $B$. Then, $p$ and $p^{\\prime}$ have a vertex in common.\n\\end{proposition}"
          }
        }
      ],
      "prior_project_context": [
        {
          "project_declarations": [
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "prop.lgv.jordan-2"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Baby Jordan curve theorem: Under NW conditions, paths must intersect.\n    (Proposition prop.lgv.jordan-2)\n    Label: prop.lgv.jordan-2\n\n    **Proof strategy** (from tex source, Section sec.details.det.comb):\n\n    The proof is by strong induction on ℓ(p) + ℓ(p'), the sum of path lengths.\n\n    **Base case**: If ℓ(p) + ℓ(p') = 0, then both paths are empty, so A = B' and A' = B.\n    From the NW conditions:\n    - A'.x ≤ A.x = B'.x ≤ B.x = A'.x, so A'.x = A.x\n    - A'.y ≥ A.y = B'.y ≥ B.y = A'.y, so A'.y = A.y\n    Thus A = A', and A is a common vertex.\n\n    **Induction step**: Assume the theorem holds for smaller path lengths.\n    If A = A', then A is a common vertex and we're done.\n    Otherwise, since A' is weakly NW of A and A ≠ A', we have either:\n    - Case 1: A'.y > A.y (A' strictly north of A)\n    - Case 2: A'.x < A.x (A' strictly west of A)\n\n    In Case 1 (A'.y > A.y):\n    - Let P be the next vertex after A on path p\n    - If the first step of p is north, then P = (A.x, A.y + 1)\n    - Since A'.y > A.y and integers, A'.y ≥ A.y + 1 = P.y\n    - So A' is still weakly NW of P\n    - The rest of p goes from P to B', and p' goes from A' to B\n    - By IH (with smaller path length), these paths intersect\n    - Any common vertex of (rest of p) and p' is also on p\n\n    - If the first step of p is east, then P = (A.x + 1, A.y)\n    - We need to look at the first step of p' and apply IH appropriately\n\n    Case 2 (A'.x < A.x) is symmetric, looking at the first step of p' instead.\n\n    The key insight is that the \"river\" created by path p from A to B' must be\n    crossed by path p' from A' to B, since A' is northwest of A and B' is\n    northwest of B.\n\n    Reference: https://math.stackexchange.com/questions/2870640/ -/\ntheorem baby_jordan_curve (A A' B B' : LatticePoint)",
              "line_range": [
                2699,
                2863
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV1",
              "name": "LGV1.baby_jordan_curve",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV1.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "sec.details.det.comb"
              ],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": true
            },
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "prop.lgv.jordan-2"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Under NW conditions, there are no nipats from (A,A') to (B',B).\n    Label: prop.lgv.jordan-2 -/\ntheorem no_nipats_under_nw (A A' B B' : LatticePoint)\n    (hA : isWeaklyNorthwestOf A' A) (hB : isWeaklyNorthwestOf B' B) :\n    numNipats2 A A' B' B = 0",
              "line_range": [
                2865,
                2901
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV1",
              "name": "LGV1.no_nipats_under_nw",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV1.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "prop.lgv.jordan-2"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Baby Jordan curve theorem (Proposition prop.lgv.jordan-2)\n    Label: prop.lgv.jordan-2\n\n    If A' is weakly northwest of A, and B' is weakly northwest of B,\n    then any path from A to B' and any path from A' to B must intersect.\n\n    This is used to show that non-identity permutations have no nipats.\n\n    **Proof sketch:**\n    Both paths visit vertices with consecutive sum values (x + y).\n    The sum ranges [A.1+A.2, B'.1+B'.2] for p and [A'.1+A'.2, B.1+B.2] for p' overlap.\n    At each sum s in the overlap, path p has x-coordinate x_p(s) and p' has x_p'(s).\n    At s_lo = max(A.1+A.2, A'.1+A'.2): x_p ≥ x_p' (because A.1 ≥ A'.1 and A.2 ≤ A'.2).\n    At s_hi = min(B'.1+B'.2, B.1+B.2): x_p ≤ x_p' (because B'.1 ≤ B.1 and B'.2 ≥ B.2).\n    By discrete IVT, there exists s where x_p(s) = x_p'(s), so the vertices coincide. -/\ntheorem baby_jordan {A A' B B' : ℤ × ℤ}\n    (hxA : xCoord A' ≤ xCoord A) (hyA : yCoord A ≤ yCoord A')\n    (hxB : xCoord B' ≤ xCoord B) (hyB : yCoord B ≤ yCoord B')\n    (p : SimpleDigraph.Path integerLattice) (hp_start : p.start = A) (hp_finish : p.finish = B')\n    (p' : SimpleDigraph.Path integerLattice) (hp'_start : p'.start = A') (hp'_finish : p'.finish = B) :\n    pathsIntersect p p'",
              "line_range": [
                3903,
                4175
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
              "name": "LGV.baby_jordan",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": true
            }
          ],
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
          "source_labels": [
            "prop.lgv.jordan-2",
            "eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.1",
            "eq.prop.lgv.jordan-2.2"
          ],
          "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex:prop.lgv.jordan-2"
        }
      ],
      "source_context_candidates": {
        "max_previous_same_file_context": 2,
        "selected_context_refs": [

        "selection_policy": "All explicit referenced source units plus the most recent same-file predecessor units up to max_previous_same_file_context."
      },
      "source_unit": {
        "environment": "corollary",
        "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex:cor.lgv.binom-unimod",
        "labels": [

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV1.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.lgv.binom-unimod}Let $n,k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Then, $\\dbinom{n}{k}%\n^{2}\\geq\\dbinom{n}{k-1}\\cdot\\dbinom{n}{k+1}$.\n\\end{corollary}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-001"
    },

      "previous_source_context": [
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "proposition",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/FPSDefinition.tex:prop.fps.xk",
            "labels": [
              "prop.fps.xk"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              762,
              769
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/FPSDefinition.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.fps.xk}We have%\n\\[\nx^{k}=\\left(  \\underbrace{0,0,\\ldots,0}_{k\\text{ times}},1,0,0,0,\\ldots\n\\right)  \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for each }k\\in\\mathbb{N}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "corollary",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/FPSDefinition.tex:cor.fps.sumakxk",
            "labels": [
              "cor.fps.sumakxk"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              802,
              811
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/FPSDefinition.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.fps.sumakxk}Any FPS $\\left(  a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},\\ldots\\right)  \\in\nK\\left[  \\left[  x\\right]  \\right]  $ satisfies%\n\\[\n\\left(  a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},\\ldots\\right)  =a_{0}+a_{1}x+a_{2}x^{2}+a_{3}%\nx^{3}+\\cdots=\\sum_{n\\in\\mathbb{N}}a_{n}x^{n}.\n\\]\nIn particular, the right hand side here is well-defined, i.e., the family\n$\\left(  a_{n}x^{n}\\right)  _{n\\in\\mathbb{N}}$ is summable.\n\\end{corollary}"
          }
        }
      ],

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/FPSDefinition.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.binom.vandermonde.NN}Let $a,b\\in\\mathbb{N}$, and let\n$n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Then,%\n\\begin{equation}\n\\dbinom{a+b}{n}=\\sum_{k=0}^{n}\\dbinom{a}{k}\\dbinom{b}{n-k}.\n\\label{eq.prop.binom.vandermonde.NN.eq}%\n\\end{equation}\n\n\\end{proposition}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-002"
    },

        "line_range": [

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/InclusionExclusion2.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Boolean M\\\"{o}bius inversion]\\label{thm.pie.moeb}Let $S$ be a finite set. Let\n$A$ be any additive abelian group.\n\nFor each subset $I$ of $S$, let $a_{I}$ and $b_{I}$ be two elements of $A$.\n\nAssume that%\n\\begin{equation}\nb_{I}=\\sum_{J\\subseteq I}a_{J}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for all }I\\subseteq S.\n\\label{eq.thm.pie.moeb.ass}%\n\\end{equation}\n\n\nThen, we also have%\n\\begin{equation}\na_{I}=\\sum_{J\\subseteq I}\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{\\left\\vert I\\setminus\nJ\\right\\vert }b_{J}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for all }I\\subseteq S.\n\\label{eq.thm.pie.moeb.claim}%\n\\end{equation}\n\n\\end{theorem}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-003"
    },

      "local_file_predecessor_declarations": [
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "structure",
          "lean_snippet": "structure NPartition (N : ℕ) where\n  /-- The entries of the N-partition as a function from `Fin N` to `ℕ` -/\n  parts : Fin N → ℕ\n  /-- The entries are weakly decreasing (antitone) -/\n  antitone : Antitone parts\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\nvariable {N : ℕ}\n\n/-! ## Compatibility aliases for field names -/\n\n/-- Alias for `antitone` to match SchurBasics.lean naming convention.\n    The weakly decreasing condition: if `i ≤ j` then `parts j ≤ parts i`. -/",
          "line_range": [
            132,
            145
          ],
          "name": "NPartition",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def size (μ : NPartition N) : ℕ := ∑ i, μ.parts i\n\n@[simp]",
          "line_range": [
            206,
            208
          ],
          "name": "size",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def length (μ : NPartition N) : ℕ := (Finset.univ.filter (fun i : Fin N => μ.parts i ≠ 0)).card\n\n@[simp]",
          "line_range": [
            453,
            455
          ],
          "name": "length",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def part (μ : NPartition N) (i : Fin N) : ℕ := μ.parts i\n\n@[simp]",
          "line_range": [
            517,
            519
          ],
          "name": "part",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem part_eq_parts (μ : NPartition N) (i : Fin N) : μ.part i = μ.parts i := rfl\n\n/-! ## Bijection with Nat.Partition (Proposition prop.sf.Npar-as-par)\n\nThere is a bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions.\nThis is formalized in MonomialSymmetric.lean as `NPartition.equivPartition`.\nHere we provide the basic conversion functions. -/\n\n/-- Helper lemma: filtering out zeros doesn't change the sum. -/",
          "line_range": [
            520,
            528
          ],
          "name": "part_eq_parts",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma filter_ne_zero_sum (m : Multiset ℕ) : (m.filter (· ≠ 0)).sum = m.sum",
          "line_range": [
            529,
            542
          ],
          "name": "filter_ne_zero_sum",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def ofPartition {n : ℕ} (p : Nat.Partition n) (_hp : Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N) : NPartition N where\n  parts := fun i =>\n    let sorted := p.parts.sort (· ≥ ·)\n    if h : i.val < sorted.length then sorted.get ⟨i.val, h⟩ else 0\n  antitone := by\n    intro i j hij\n    simp only\n    split_ifs with hi hj hj\n    · -- Both in range: use that sorted list is decreasing\n      have hsorted : (p.parts.sort (· ≥ ·)).Pairwise (· ≥ ·) :=\n        Multiset.pairwise_sort (r := (· ≥ ·)) p.parts\n      exact List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_le hsorted hij\n    · -- i in range, j out of range: contradiction since i ≤ j\n      omega\n    · -- i out of range: 0 ≤ anything\n      exact Nat.zero_le _\n    · -- both out of range\n      exact le_refl 0\n\n/-- Convert an N-partition to a partition by removing trailing zeros. -/",
          "line_range": [
            543,
            562
          ],
          "name": "ofPartition",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; the hidden target declaration itself is omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def toPartition (μ : NPartition N) : Nat.Partition μ.size where\n  parts := (Finset.univ.val.map μ.parts).filter (· ≠ 0)\n  parts_pos := by\n    intro i hi\n    simp only [Multiset.mem_filter, Multiset.mem_map, Finset.mem_val, Finset.mem_univ,\n      true_and] at hi\n    omega\n  parts_sum := by\n    simp only [size]\n    have h : (Finset.univ.val.map μ.parts).sum = ∑ i, μ.parts i := by\n      simp [Finset.sum_eq_multiset_sum]\n    rw [← h]\n    exact filter_ne_zero_sum _",
          "line_range": [
            563,

            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/MonomialSymmetric.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{definition}\n\\label{def.sf.Npar}An $N$\\emph{-partition} will mean a weakly decreasing\n$N$-tuple of nonnegative integers. In other words, an $N$-partition means an\n$N$-tuple $\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{N}\\right)\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ with $\\lambda_{1}\\geq\\lambda_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq\\lambda_{N}$.\n\\end{definition}"
          }
        }
      ],

              "kind": "abbrev",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Alias for the canonical `NPartition` type within this namespace.\n    An N-partition is a weakly decreasing N-tuple of nonnegative integers.\n    (Definition def.sf.Npar)\n\n    This is represented as a function `Fin N → ℕ` that is antitone\n    (i.e., `i ≤ j → parts j ≤ parts i`).\n\n    All basic operations (`size`, `length`, `zero`, etc.) and lemmas are inherited\n    from the canonical `_root_.NPartition` definition in `NPartition.lean`. -/\nabbrev NPartition (N : ℕ) := _root_.NPartition N\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\n-- All basic definitions and lemmas (ext, DecidableEq, size, zero, length, etc.)\n-- are inherited from _root_.NPartition in NPartition.lean.\n\n/-!\n## Bijection with partitions of length ≤ N (Proposition prop.sf.Npar-as-par)\n\nThere is a bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions.\n\nThe core definitions (`ofPartition`, `toPartition`, `ofPartition_size`, `toPartition_card_le`)\nare defined in `NPartition.lean` and re-exported here for convenience within this namespace.\n-/\n\n-- Re-export canonical definitions from _root_.NPartition to this namespace.\n-- This allows code in this file to use `ofPartition`, `toPartition`, etc.\n-- without qualification, while avoiding code duplication.",

              "kind": "structure",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- An N-partition is a weakly decreasing N-tuple of nonnegative integers.\n    (Definition def.sf.Npar)\n\n    This is represented as a function `Fin N → ℕ` that is antitone\n    (i.e., `i ≤ j → parts j ≤ parts i`).\n\n    The field is named `antitone` to match Mathlib conventions. -/\nstructure NPartition (N : ℕ) where\n  /-- The entries of the N-partition as a function from `Fin N` to `ℕ` -/\n  parts : Fin N → ℕ\n  /-- The entries are weakly decreasing (antitone) -/\n  antitone : Antitone parts\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\nvariable {N : ℕ}\n\n/-! ## Compatibility aliases for field names -/",

        "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.sf.Npar-as-par}There is a bijection%\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left\\{  \\text{partitions of length }\\leq N\\right\\}   &  \\rightarrow\\left\\{\nN\\text{-partitions}\\right\\}  ,\\\\\n\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{\\ell}\\right)   &\n\\mapsto\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{\\ell}%\n,\\underbrace{0,0,\\ldots,0}_{N-\\ell\\text{ zeroes}}\\right)  .\n\\end{align*}\n\n\\end{proposition}"
      },
